meaning: v.把...解剖(动植物等),切开仔细研究,把...切成碎片
examples: People want to dissect his work and question his motives.
He watched her peel and dissect a pear neatly, no mess, no sticky fingers.
In biology class we had to dissect a frog.
To properly dissect disruptive content paths, we first need to take a step back.
You can dissect a balance sheet until you dream of numbers dancing in mini-skirts.
